messages.content:
### Task:
Respond to the user query using the provided context, incorporating inline citations in the format [id] **only when the <source> tag includes an explicit id attribute** (e.g., <source id="1">).

### Guidelines:
- If you don't know the answer, clearly state that.
- If uncertain, ask the user for clarification.
- Respond in the same language as the user's query.
- If the context is unreadable or of poor quality, inform the user and provide the best possible answer.
- If the answer isn't present in the context but you possess the knowledge, explain this to the user and provide the answer using your own understanding.
- **Only include inline citations using [id] (e.g., [1], [2]) when the <source> tag includes an id attribute.**
- Do not cite if the <source> tag does not contain an id attribute.
- Do not use XML tags in your response.
- Ensure citations are concise and directly related to the information provided.

### Example of Citation:
If the user asks about a specific topic and the information is found in a source with a provided id attribute, the response should include the citation like in the following example:
* "According to the study, the proposed method increases efficiency by 20% [1]."

### Output:
Provide a clear and direct response to the user's query, including inline citations in the format [id] only when the <source> tag with id attribute is present in the context.

<context>

Извлеки из этих файлов метаданные (руководитель, дата выгрузки) и саму таблицу с транзакциями (дата, артикул, стоимость). Игнорируй строки итогов. Результат представь в виде JSON

function.name: get_current_timestamp
function.description: Get the current Unix timestamp in seconds.

function.name: calculate_timestamp
function.description:
Get the current Unix timestamp, optionally adjusted by days, weeks, months, or years.
Use this to calculate timestamps for date filtering in search functions.
Examples: "last week" = weeks_ago=1, "3 days ago" = days_ago=3, "a year ago" = years_ago=1

days_ago.description: Number of days to subtract from current time (default: 0)

weeks_ago.description: Number of weeks to subtract from current time (default: 0)

months_ago.description: Number of months to subtract from current time (default: 0)

years_ago.description: Number of years to subtract from current time (default: 0)

function.name: list_knowledge_bases
function.description:
List the user's accessible knowledge bases so a relevant internal source
can be chosen.

count.description: Maximum number of KBs to return (default: 10)

skip.description: Number of results to skip for pagination (default: 0)

function.name: search_knowledge_bases
function.description:
Search the user's accessible knowledge bases by name and description to find
a relevant internal source.

query.description: The search query to find matching knowledge bases

count.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 5)

skip.description: Number of results to skip for pagination (default: 0)

function.name: query_knowledge_bases
function.description:
Search knowledge bases by semantic similarity to query.
Finds KBs whose name/description match the meaning of your query.
Helpful for discovering which knowledge base to query next.

query.description: Natural language query describing what you're looking for

function.name: grep_knowledge_files
function.description:
Search for exact text across knowledge files. Returns matching lines with line numbers.
Unlike query_knowledge_files (semantic/vector search), this performs exact string matching.
Automatically detects regex patterns (e.g. "error|warn", "version \d+").
Helpful for literal strings, identifiers, error messages, or regex-style searches.

pattern.description: The text pattern to search for (regex auto-detected)

file_id.description: Optional file ID to search within a single file only

case_insensitive.description: If true, ignore case when matching (default: false)

count_only.description: If true, return only match counts per file (default: false)

function.name: search_knowledge_files
function.description:
Search files by filename across knowledge bases the user has access to.
When the model has attached knowledge, searches only within attached KBs and files.
Helpful when looking for a specific document or file name.

query.description: The search query to find matching files by filename

knowledge_id.description: Optional KB id to limit search to a specific knowledge base

count.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 5)

skip.description: Number of results to skip for pagination (default: 0)

function.name: query_knowledge_files
function.description:
Search knowledge base files using semantic/vector search. Searches across collections (KBs),
individual files, and notes that the user has access to.
Helpful for internal documentation, uploaded knowledge, and attached model knowledge.

query.description: The search query to find semantically relevant content

knowledge_ids.description: Optional list of KB ids to limit search to specific knowledge bases

count.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 5)

function.name: view_knowledge_file
function.description: Get the content of a file from a knowledge base. Supports pagination for large files.

file_id.description: The ID of the file to retrieve

offset.description: Character offset to start reading from (default: 0)

max_chars.description: Maximum characters to return (default: 10000, hard cap: 100000)

line_numbers.description: If true, prefix each line with its 1-indexed line number

start_line.description: Optional 1-indexed start line (overrides offset/max_chars when set)

function.name: search_chats
function.description:
Search the user's previous chat conversations by title and message content.
Helpful for finding details from earlier conversations.

query.description: The search query to find matching chats

count.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 5)

start_timestamp.description: Only include chats updated after this Unix timestamp (seconds)

end_timestamp.description: Only include chats updated before this Unix timestamp (seconds)

function.name: view_chat
function.description:
Get the full conversation history of a chat by its ID after a relevant
previous chat has been identified.

chat_id.description: The ID of the chat to retrieve

function.name: search_memories
function.description: Search or browse saved memories by content, path, type, or memory ID.

query.description: Optional query to search memory content and path

path.description: Optional memory path to search around

memory_id.description: Optional exact memory ID to read

function.name: list_memory_paths
function.description: List saved memory paths to find existing memory groups before writing or moving memories.

query.description: Optional query to filter memory paths or contents

count.description: Maximum number of paths to return

function.name: read_memory_path
function.description: Read saved memories at a memory path, including nearby parent and child paths.

count.description: Maximum number of memories to return

function.name: list_memories
function.description: List all stored memories for the user, including IDs and timestamps.

function.name: update_memory

operations.description: Memory operations to apply in one request

function.name: add_memory
function.description:
Save enduring information that can improve future chats.

Save stable preferences, goals, projects, relationships, habits, and standing instructions.
Do not save one-off activity, meals, routine daily events, temporary mood, or other short-lived details
unless the user explicitly asks you to remember them.

type.description: Use "user" for facts/preferences about the user, or "context" for other durable context

path.description: Optional stable memory address for grouping related memories

function.name: replace_memory_content
function.description: Update an existing saved memory by its ID when its content needs correction.

memory_id.description: The ID of the memory to update

content.description: The new content for the memory

type.description: Optional "user" or "context" type for the updated memory

path.description: Optional stable memory address for grouping related memories

function.name: delete_memory
function.description: Delete a saved memory by its ID.

memory_id.description: The ID of the memory to delete

function.name: search_notes
function.description: Search the user's saved notes by title and content.

query.description: The search query to find matching notes

count.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 5)

start_timestamp.description: Only include notes updated after this Unix timestamp (seconds)

end_timestamp.description: Only include notes updated before this Unix timestamp (seconds)

function.name: view_note
function.description: Get the full content of a note by its ID.

note_id.description: The ID of the note to retrieve

function.name: write_note
function.description: Create a new note with the given title and content.

title.description: The title of the new note

content.description: The markdown content for the note

function.name: replace_note_content
function.description: Update the markdown content, and optionally the title, of an existing note.

note_id.description: The ID of the note to update

content.description: The new markdown content for the note

title.description: Optional new title for the note

function.name: create_tasks
function.description: Create a visible task checklist for multi-step work so progress can be shown in chat.

tasks.description: List of task items. Each item: content (string, required), status (pending|in_progress|completed|cancelled, default pending), id (optional, auto-generated).

id.description: Unique identifier for the task. Auto-generated if omitted.

function.name: update_task
function.description: Mark a single visible task item as completed, in_progress, pending, or cancelled.

status.description: New status: completed, in_progress, pending, or cancelled (default: completed)

function.name: create_automation
function.description:
Create a scheduled automation that runs a prompt on a recurring or one-time schedule.
Use this when the user wants to schedule a task to run automatically.
The automation will use the current chat model.

The rrule parameter must be a valid iCalendar RRULE string. Common examples:
- Every day at 9am: "DTSTART:20250101T090000\nRRULE:FREQ=DAILY"
- Every Monday at 8am: "DTSTART:20250106T080000\nRRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO"
- Every hour: "RRULE:FREQ=HOURLY;INTERVAL=1"
- Every 30 minutes: "RRULE:FREQ=MINUTELY;INTERVAL=30"
- Once at a specific time: "DTSTART:20250415T140000\nRRULE:FREQ=DAILY;COUNT=1"
- First day of every month: "DTSTART:20250101T090000\nRRULE:FREQ=MONTHLY;BYMONTHDAY=1"

The DTSTART time should reflect the desired execution time. Use COUNT=1 for one-time automations.

name.description: A short descriptive name for the automation

prompt.description: The prompt/instructions to execute on each run

rrule.description: An iCalendar RRULE string defining the schedule

function.name: update_automation
function.description: Update an existing automation. Only the provided fields are changed; omitted fields stay the same.

automation_id.description: The ID of the automation to update

rrule.description: New iCalendar RRULE schedule string (optional). See create_automation for format examples.

function.name: list_automations
function.description: List the user's scheduled automations.

status.description: Filter by status: "active", "paused", or omit for all

count.description: Maximum number of automations to return (default: 10)

function.name: toggle_automation
function.description: Pause or resume a scheduled automation. If active, it will be paused. If paused, it will be resumed.

automation_id.description: The ID of the automation to toggle

function.name: delete_automation
function.description: Delete a scheduled automation and all its run history.

automation_id.description: The ID of the automation to delete

function.name: search_calendar_events
function.description:
Search calendar events, reminders, and scheduled items by text and/or date range.
Helpful for finding upcoming events, reminders, or schedule items.

query.description: Search text to match against event title, description, or location (optional)

start.description: Only return events starting at or after this datetime, e.g. "2026-04-20 00:00" (optional)

end.description: Only return events starting before this datetime, e.g. "2026-04-27 00:00" (optional)

count.description: Maximum number of events to return (default: 10)

function.name: create_calendar_event
function.description:
Create a calendar event, reminder, or alarm. Use this when the user wants to
schedule an event, set a reminder, create an alarm, or says things like
"remind me", "don't let me forget", "notify me at", or "add to my calendar".
For simple reminders, omit end/location/all_day and set reminder_minutes to 0.

title.description: Event or reminder title (e.g. "Team standup", "Take medicine", "Call mom")

start.description: Start datetime in the user's local time (e.g. "2026-04-20 09:00")

end.description: End datetime in the user's local time (optional — omit for reminders or point-in-time events)

calendar_id.description: Target calendar ID (optional, uses default calendar if omitted)

all_day.description: Whether this is an all-day event (default: false)

reminder_minutes.description: Minutes before the event to send a notification (optional, default: 10). Use 0 for "at time of event", -1 for no notification.

function.name: update_calendar_event
function.description:
Update an existing calendar event. Only provided fields are changed;
omitted fields stay the same.

event_id.description: The ID of the event to update

start.description: New start datetime string in your local time, e.g. "2026-04-20 09:00" (optional)

end.description: New end datetime string in your local time (optional)

all_day.description: Whether this is an all-day event (optional)

is_cancelled.description: Set to true to cancel the event (optional)

reminder_minutes.description: Minutes before the event to send a reminder notification (optional). Use 0 for "at time of event", -1 for no reminder. Accepts any positive integer for custom timing (e.g. 120 for 2 hours before).

function.name: delete_calendar_event
function.description: Delete a calendar event permanently.

event_id.description: The ID of the event to delete
